videos.title: Never Mix Up Australia and South Africa Again
How To Always Guess the Correct State in Australia

hints.text: **Gum trees (eucalyptus)** dominate: light bark, puffy leaf clusters (look varies by region); drives on the **left**
Bollards white w/ **red front reflector / grey back**

shared_with.note: NZ's red reflector WRAPS around the back edge-to-edge.

hints.text: Road lines almost exclusively **all white** (rare alpine exceptions like Kosciuszko/Hotham); outer lines sometimes w/ perpendicular **rumble-strip stripes** (very rare in NT)

shared_with.note: Yellow MIDDLE lines → NZ (but NZ can be all-white too; NZ has similar rumble strips).

hints.text: Give-way signs: white border + **black text**; speed signs: red circle **on a large white rectangle**

shared_with.note: NZ give-way text is RED; NZ speed signs lack the white rectangle.

hints.text: Chevrons **white-on-black + black-on-yellow**; green signs w/ white arrows + town distances at intersections
**State plate designs** partly readable through the blur; **2-digit phone area codes** map to states; each state has a unique **bus-stop design** (Darwin: concrete shelters; Tasmania: 'metro')

bullets: Warramboo (SA) → Madura (WA): dry shrubs + wide-crowned eucalyptus, sandy orange soil (whiter east of Adelaide→NW Victoria, redder on the WA side)
Yorke Peninsula → Echuca band: lush green/golden fields (SA side drier; Vic side taller leafier trees)
Coastal N-Queensland: tropical — palms + dark green + SUGARCANE
Inland NSW: flat, redder inland, pale-green dry shrubs
Darwin area: dark red soil + rich green grass + BLACK tree trunks
Tasmania: cool temperate green rolling hills + eucalyptus forests (NZ/Chile look)
Termite mounds: NT (rarely N-WA/QLD)
Brush plants: Western Australia

hints.text: **Stobie poles** (steel + concrete, trident or rotated-'E' tops) are the SA icon (a few thousand strayed to other states, esp. Tasmania); short bollard reflectors common (also QLD); white property markers; flat signposts (also WA); SA bus stops have yellow posts
**Rusty metal poles w/ holes** (also Wyndham, N-WA); **130 km/h limits = NT only**; yellow signpost stickers; **no Gen2 in NT**

**Yellow signposts** = WA-specific; **black-white striped bollards** unique to WA; brown-bottomed bollards; poles w/ green-painted bases + white sticker/red-white band; trident poletops w/ red insulators + black crossbar (also Vic); dark-green property markers

~**64% of Australia's concrete poles** are in Victoria; poletops w/ **3 circular coils** (2 sides + 1 top); 'C' roads only Vic/Tas (+SA border); white hydrant poles w/ blue circle + red top; fire hydrants w/ red-painted tops (unique)

Poletops w/ **upward-angled coils** (unique); circular **blue pole stickers**; black pole markings + diagonal numbers → Gold Coast/Brisbane/Sunshine Coast; **fluorescent-border signs** ≈ QLD-only; black-yellow bollard exclusive to QLD
Thin metal **L-shaped crossbars** (mainland uses wood); **olive-green possum guards** on poles (colour = Tas-only); **white directional signs** = only Tasmania

**Red 'No Stopping' signs** = NSW only (others white; ACT's white ones w/ text = ACT-unique); guardrail endings w/ black dash on yellow ≈ NSW/ACT; thin arrow-less 'Keep Left' → NSW/QLD/NT; most **snorkel cars** → NSW/ACT (only 2 in WA)
**Traffic lights**: white outline around the frame → WA/QLD/NSW/ACT/Tas; no border → the central states (SA/NT/Vic)
**Christmas Island & Cocos Islands count as Australia** for streaks — both have unique cars (own pages on this site)

bullets: Tom Price (WA): red soil + Gen2 + rocky hills
Alice Springs (NT): orange rocks at road ends
Cairns (QLD): tropical + big green mountains + high-rises & divided roads
Grampians (Vic): lone hills over flat farmland
Mount Gambier (SA): white soil + pine plantations
Kangaroo Island (SA): stubby eucalyptus + WA-like orange soil, only Gen2+Gen4
Stirling Ranges (WA): lone range over a flat plain
Nullarbor Hwy: treeless grey flatness
Barkly Hwy (NT + a bit of QLD): wire on the left of the car
Dorat Road (NT): Darwin landscape + no road lines
Sturt Hwy (Wentworth↔Wagga Wagga): triple broken centre lines
Stuart Hwy (Alice→Pimba): bright orange dirt + blue Gen4 car (NT side: barely any rumble strips)
Mount Kosciuszko: ALL-YELLOW lines + white snorkel truck w/ cab guard
Broken Hill (NSW): stray SA-style Stobie poles

hints.text: **City street signs**: Melbourne poles = white rectangular sticker w/ small blue square; unique council designs for **Brisbane**, **Gold Coast** and **Sydney**
